response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack/web engineer with strong testing discipline and broad language exposure, but the role is a specialized embedded-security position requiring native Japanese, deep C on UNIX, Linux kernel modification experience, and switch/networking security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP). The resume shows no Japanese proficiency, no embedded LAN-switch or kernel-level work, and only brief C/C++ exposure from a decade ago at National Instruments.
strengths: Long track record of shipping production software with strong testing/TDD discipline
Comfortable across many languages and stacks, including some C/C++ and Linux experience
Active open-source contributor and community educator, signaling self-driven learning
concerns: No native (or any documented) Japanese language proficiency — a hard requirement
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, or Linux-kernel modification experience
No demonstrated work in security domains required (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF/SNMP, chip-vendor SDKs)
